calendar.matters:
Petition For Determination That Wife Lacks Capacity To Join In Or Consent To Proposed Transaction; And Authorization To Execute And Fund Revocable Tru
Off Calendar Sep-13-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Charlotte Ito for Takao Kosai (Petitioner).

The Court questions the search for relatives within the second degree. Ms. Ito stated that she filed a declaration by Mr. Kosai, that he attempted to but could no longer find any relatives in Japan, and that the only persons provided with notice were his Son and his Grandchildren. Ms. Ito also stated that there were two assets, that all of the assets are in both names, that there is a bank account and the residence, and that she will strike the clause with regard to designation of the community property. Ms. Ito is to provide a revised order. The Court granted the Petition for Determination that Wife lacks Capacity to Join in or Consent to Proposed Transaction (subject to receipt and review of the revised order).

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Wu. Not reported.

Petition For Determination That Wife Lacks Capacity To Join In Or Consent To Proposed Transaction; And Authorization To Execute And Fund Revocable Tru
Off Calendar Aug-22-2018 Continued To Sep-05-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Charlotte Ito for Takao Kosai (Petitioner).

The Court stated that everything with the petition seems to be in order, that there are some issues with the Trust, that they are asking the Court to determine that all of the property is community property, and asked how title of the real property and the bank accounts are held. Ms. Ito stated that this couple has been married for decades, that all of the property is in Takao and Hiromi's names, and that she brought copies of the real estate deeds and the bank accounts. Ms. Ito also stated that the Petitioner is retired, that the Wife is bedridden and that it is all of the money the Petitioner has worked for. In addition, Ms. Ito referred to provisions 21.5 and 21.1, stated that after reading the Probate Examiner's notes, she sees why the intent to say that the Trust shall permit the surviving spouse to reside in the residence is ambiguous because the Trustee has the right to sell, and that perhaps she will strike that. The Court directed Ms. Ito to file the above mentioned documents, to make the changes to the Trust document and to attach it the proposed order. The Petition for Determination That Wife Lacks Capacity to Join In or Consent to Proposed Transaction, etc. is continued to September 5, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.
